summary: Moved the Dolphin DSP header from the source tree into the public include tree and updated its include guard and include style accordingly. The implementation file now includes the header as a Dolphin SDK-style public include.
what_changed: Changed the header guard from `_DSP_H_` to `__DOLPHIN_DSP_H_`.
Updated `src/dolphin/dsp/dsp.c` to include the header with angle brackets: `#include <dolphin/dsp/dsp.h>` instead of `#include "dolphin/dsp/dsp.h"`.
smart_moves: Placed the DSP header under `include/dolphin/dsp/`, matching the apparent public Dolphin SDK include hierarchy rather than keeping it local to `src/dolphin/dsp/`.
Used a more namespace-specific include guard, `__DOLPHIN_DSP_H_`, reducing ambiguity compared with `_DSP_H_`.
Switched to angle-bracket include syntax for a header now treated as part of the public include tree.
decomp_lessons: When reconstructing Dolphin SDK headers, prefer organizing them under `include/dolphin/<subsystem>/` when they represent public SDK interfaces.
Header guards should encode the subsystem path when possible, e.g. `__DOLPHIN_DSP_H_`, to reflect SDK-style naming and avoid collisions.
After moving a header from `src` to `include`, update C files to use the public include form so future files can consume it consistently.
naming_conventions: Dolphin subsystem headers may use guards of the form `__DOLPHIN_<SUBSYSTEM>_H_`.
Public Dolphin headers are included as `<dolphin/subsystem/header.h>`.
The PR retained the existing DSP symbol spelling visible in the diff, including `__DSP_init_flag`, but did not otherwise change symbol names.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin SDK headers still located under `src/dolphin` that should be moved to `include/dolphin`.
Search for local quoted includes of Dolphin public headers that should use angle brackets.
Check for generic header guards in Dolphin headers that should be renamed to subsystem-specific SDK-style guards.
